data.name: CommandInteractiveOptionDescription
data.value: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action.

data.name: InfoOptionDescription
data.value: Display dotnetup information.

data.name: InfoCommandDescription
data.value: Display dotnetup information.

data.name: FormatOptionDescription
data.value: Output format (text or json).

data.name: InfoHeader
data.value: dotnetup Information:

data.name: InfoNoListOptionDescription
data.value: Skip listing installed SDKs (faster).

data.name: ListCommandDescription
data.value: List installed .NET SDKs and runtimes managed by dotnetup.

data.name: ListNoVerifyOptionDescription
data.value: Skip verifying that each installation is valid.

data.name: ListHeaderTitle
data.value: Installations

data.name: ListHeaderSubtitle
data.value: (managed by dotnetup)

data.name: ListNoInstallations
data.value: (none)

data.name: ListTotal
data.value: Total

data.name: RootCommandDescription
data.value: .NET toolchain manager for user level installs.

data.name: DotnetCommandDescription
data.value: Run a command using the dotnetup-managed dotnet installation.

data.name: DotnetCommandForwardedArgsDescription
data.value: Arguments to forward to dotnet.

data.name: DotnetCommandDotnetNotFound
data.value: Error: dotnet executable not found at '{0}'.

data.name: DotnetCommandInstallFirst
data.value: Run 'dotnetup install' to install a .NET SDK first.

data.name: DotnetCommandDotnetStartFailed
data.value: Error: Failed to start the dotnet process.

data.name: TelemetryNotice
data.value: dotnetup collects usage data to help improve your experience. You can opt out by setting the DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT environment variable to '1'. Learn more: https://aka.ms/dotnetup-telemetry

data.name: InitCommandDescription
data.value: Configure or reconfigure dotnetup settings and install .NET through the interactive setup flow.

data.name: AccessModeNone
data.value: Isolation Mode

data.name: AccessModeShell
data.value: Terminal Mode

data.name: PathPreferenceSuggestedSuffix
data.value: (Suggested)
data.comment: Hint appended after the recommended mode name in the init summary, e.g. "Terminal Mode (Suggested)".

data.name: AccessModeEverywhere
data.value: Replacement Mode

data.name: PathGuidanceNone
data.value: You can run .NET commands with 'dotnetup dotnet <command>' (or 'dotnetup do <command>' for short).

data.name: PathGuidanceShell
data.value: Your shell profile has been updated. Restart your terminal or source your profile to use 'dotnet' directly.

data.name: PathGuidanceEverywhere
data.value: PATH and DOTNET_ROOT have been updated. You can use 'dotnet' directly in new terminal sessions.

data.name: PathDescriptionNone
data.value: Use 'dotnetup dotnet' to consume installs managed by dotnetup. New installs can be used alongside your existing installs.

data.name: PathDescriptionShell
data.value: Configure the current shell profile to use installs managed by dotnetup. Only applications launched from the shell will leverage dotnetup installs.

data.name: PathDescriptionShellBase
data.value: Configure the current shell profile to use installs managed by dotnetup.

data.name: PathDescriptionEverywhere
data.value: The system will be configured to use dotnetup installs over any other installs.

data.name: PathTooltipNone
data.value: Recommended for users without admin rights, or for users who want to keep their system managed installs (e.g. {0}) as the default.

data.name: PathTooltipShell
data.value: Modify the current shell profile file to consume dotnetup on PATH environment variables. Recommended for users who want to develop using dotnetup and launch their IDE through the terminal.

data.name: PathTooltipShellWindowsNote
data.value: Only PowerShell will work in this mode. CMD has no profile file.

data.name: PathTooltipEverywhere
data.value: Recommended for users who want all of their debuggers, applications, and IDEs to use dotnetup installs by default. Admin privileges are required. Enables 'cmd' functionality by default. Other user accounts on this machine will be impacted and applications may break.

data.name: PathReplacementModeUnixError
data.value: Replacement Mode is not supported on this operating system. Please choose Isolation Mode or Terminal Mode instead.

data.name: HelpOptionsTitle
data.value: Options:

data.name: HelpDescriptionLabel
data.value: Description:

data.name: HelpUsageLabel
data.value: Usage:

data.name: HelpInstallCommandsTitle
data.value: Install Commands:

data.name: HelpQueryCommandsTitle
data.value: Query Commands:

data.name: HelpConfigCommandsTitle
data.value: Configuration Commands:

data.name: HelpUtilityCommandsTitle
data.value: Utility Commands:

data.name: EnvScriptCommandDescription
data.value: Generates a shell script that configures the environment for .NET. With no selection flags it follows your configured env settings; pass --dotnet and/or --dotnetup to emit only those parts.

data.name: EnvScriptDotnetInstallPathOptionDescription
data.value: The path to the .NET installation directory. If not specified, uses the default user install path.

data.name: EnvScriptDotnetOptionDescription
data.value: Add the managed dotnet to PATH and set DOTNET_ROOT.

data.name: EnvScriptDotnetupOptionDescription
data.value: Add the dotnetup directory to PATH.

data.name: EnvScriptDotnetupOnlyOptionDescription
data.value: Only add dotnetup to PATH. Do not set DOTNET_ROOT or add the .NET install path.

data.name: EnvOutcomeDotnetupOnlyOnPath
data.value: dotnetup is on your PATH. dotnet is not — run it with 'dotnetup dotnet <command>'.

data.name: EnvOutcomeNeitherOnPath
data.value: Neither dotnet nor dotnetup is on your PATH.

data.name: EnvOutcomeBothOnPath
data.value: dotnet and dotnetup are on your PATH.

data.name: EnvOutcomeDotnetOnlyOnPath
data.value: dotnet is on your PATH. dotnetup is not.

data.name: EnvApplyToTerminalPrompt
EnvOpenNewTerminalToTakeEffect
data.value: Open a new terminal for the change to take effect.

data.name: EnvOpenNewTerminalForOtherSurfaces
data.value: Other open terminals and applications will pick up this change after they're restarted.

data.name: EnvShellRequiredForProfile
data.value: Could not detect the current shell, which is required to update the dotnetup profile entry. Re-run with --shell <{0}> to specify it explicitly.

data.name: EnvElevationCancelled
data.value: Elevation was cancelled. The system PATH was not modified.

data.name: EnvSetNoStoredConfig
data.value: No mode specified and no stored configuration to re-apply. Specify a mode: none, shell, or everywhere.

data.name: EnvSetConfigUnreadable
data.value: No mode specified and the existing configuration could not be read. Specify a mode explicitly: none, shell, or everywhere.

data.name: EnvModeWindowsOnly
data.value: '{0}' mode is only supported on Windows. Use 'shell' on this platform.

data.name: EnvSetUnknownMode
data.value: Unknown env mode '{0}'. Expected one of: {1}.

data.name: EnvSetModeArgumentDescription
data.value: The dotnet access mode: {0}. Omit to re-apply the stored mode.

data.name: EnvSetDotnetupOnPathOptionDescription
data.value: Whether the dotnetup directory is on PATH ('true' or 'false'). Omit to leave unchanged.

data.name: EnvSetCommandDescription
data.value: Apply (or re-sync) the configured env settings.

data.name: EnvClearCommandDescription
data.value: Remove all dotnetup environment wiring (equivalent to 'env set none --dotnetup-on-path false').

data.name: EnvCommandDescription
data.value: Manage the dotnetup environment configuration (PATH and DOTNET_ROOT wiring).

data.name: EnvShowCommandDescription
data.value: Show the configured env mode and report any detected drift.

data.name: EnvDriftProfileBlockMissing
data.value: Shell profile is missing the dotnetup managed block.

data.name: EnvDriftProfileBlockUnexpected
data.value: Shell profile contains a dotnetup managed block but neither dotnet access nor dotnetup-on-PATH is configured.

data.name: EnvDriftEverywhereModeEnvVarsIncomplete
data.value: Windows user PATH / DOTNET_ROOT / system PATH do not match 'everywhere' mode expectations.

data.name: EnvDriftEverywhereModeEnvVarsUnexpected
data.value: Windows user PATH / DOTNET_ROOT still has 'everywhere'-mode wiring (expected dotnet access: '{0}').

data.name: EnvDriftDotnetupOnPathMissing
data.value: dotnetup is configured to be on PATH but is missing from the user PATH.

data.name: EnvDriftDotnetupOnPathUnexpected
data.value: dotnetup is on the user PATH but is configured to be off.

data.name: EnvShowNoConfiguration
data.value: No dotnetup env configuration found. Run 'dotnetup env set <mode>' to configure.

data.name: EnvShowHeader
data.value: dotnetup environment:

data.name: EnvShowLabelDotnetAccess
data.value: dotnet access

data.name: EnvShowLabelDotnetupOnPath
data.value: dotnetup on PATH

data.name: EnvShowInSync
data.value: In sync.

data.name: EnvShowInSyncNotActive
data.value: In sync, but not yet active in this terminal.

data.name: EnvShowApplyToTerminalPrompt
data.value: To apply it to this terminal now, run:

data.name: EnvShowOrOpenNewTerminal
data.value: Or open a new terminal.

data.name: EnvShowOpenNewTerminalToPickUp
data.value: Open a new terminal to pick up the change.

data.name: EnvShowDriftHeader
data.value: Detected drift between configured settings and the current environment:

data.name: EnvShowRunSetToResync
data.value: Run 'dotnetup env set' to re-sync.

data.name: EnvScriptShellNotDetected
data.value: Error: Unable to detect current shell. The SHELL environment variable is not set.

data.name: EnvScriptSpecifyShellWithSupported
data.value: Please specify the shell using --shell option. Supported shells: {0}

data.name: EnvScriptUnsupportedShell
data.value: Error: Unsupported shell '{0}'.

data.name: EnvScriptSupportedShells
data.value: Supported shells: {0}

data.name: EnvScriptSpecifyShell
data.value: Please specify the shell using --shell option.

data.name: EnvScriptShellNotSet
data.value: SHELL environment variable is not set; cannot detect shell.

data.name: EnvScriptUnsupportedShellError
data.value: Unsupported shell '{0}'.

data.name: EnvScriptDotnetupOnlyConflict
data.value: --dotnetup-only cannot be combined with --dotnet or --dotnetup.

data.name: SummaryPrompt
data.value: Would you like to install .NET with the recommended settings?

data.name: SummaryProceedTitleConfigured
data.value: Yes, override settings with defaults

data.name: SummaryProceedTitleUnconfigured
data.value: Yes, proceed with defaults and install

data.name: SummaryProceedDescriptionConfigured
data.value: Replace your current settings with the recommended ones above.

data.name: SummaryProceedDescriptionUnconfigured
data.value: Install with the recommended settings shown above.

data.name: SummaryCustomizeTitle
data.value: No, customize setup

data.name: SummaryCustomizeDescription
data.value: Choose the channel, mode, and migrations yourself.

data.name: SummaryExitTitle
data.value: Exit without changes

data.name: SummaryExitDescription
data.value: Make no changes and quit.

data.name: SummaryWelcome
data.value: Welcome to {0}!

data.name: SummaryChannelLabel
data.value: SDK Channel:

data.name: SummaryChannelGlobalJsonSuffix
data.value: determined from global.json at {0}
data.comment: {0} is a file path. Shown in parentheses after the channel name.

data.name: SummaryModeLabel
data.value: Mode:

data.name: SummaryModeCurrent
data.value: current: {0}
data.comment: {0} is the user's currently configured mode name. Shown in parentheses after the recommended mode.

data.name: SummaryMigrateHeader
data.value: System installs to migrate:

data.name: SummaryMigrateMore
data.value: ... and {0} more
data.comment: {0} is the count of additional migration candidates not individually listed.
